---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

FlowCore Guide: Miscellaneous 

Washing Lab Glassware 

Put Items in Washer 

Place bottles on rack 

Make sure to put caps in a cage on the rack 

Turn on Washer (via On/Off switch) 

Select Cleaning Program 

Usually run program 4 “Wash with Short Dry” 

Selecting “f4” will take you to program selection screen 

Run Wash 

Click start (F4)  on selection screen 

Press Start button 

Autoclaving Items 

Tape caps/tops of items to be autoclaved with fresh autoclave tape 

Place items in the autoclave 

Make sure any caps are loosely on bottles 

Run correct cycle 

Bottles with liquid à Liquid Cycle 

No liquid in containers à Gravity Cycle 

Remove items from autoclave 

Make sure the chamber pressure is 0 

Make sure lines on autoclaved tape have changed from beige to black 

If lines have not changed, the autoclave did not get hot enough and the items are NOT sterile 

Tell Kathy (or someone in the office) about the autoclave so she can have it serviced 

Leave caps slightly loose until bottle cools down 

Biohazard Trash 

Requesting Bags 

Go to EHS Website 

Click on “Hazardous Material Management 

Graphical user interface, text, application, email

Description automatically generated 

Click on link to fill out special medical waste removal form 

Graphical user interface

Description automatically generated with medium confidence 

Fill out the form with all required information 

Do not fill out any information for the drop down “type” menus 

Instead, fill out a note in the “Other” category requesting a box of biohazard bags 

Graphical user interface, text, application, email

Description automatically generated 

Submit Request 

Voicemail 

Password: Flow (3569) 

Service Contracts 

Located in drawer on bottom right of the desk by the hood 

Use for easy way to look up phone number and the instrument serial number needed for a service call 

Equipment List 

Copy is on staff drive 

Update as equipment is added to, or removed from, core 

MACSQuant Running Buffer 

Dilute from 16X buffer 

Add H2O first, then add buffer concentrate 

Make up to 4 bottles at a time 

Note: Bottles are 1.5 L NOT 1 L
